Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the word "वाईट" (waiṭa), which means "bad" or "worst," indicating a negative situation. The emotion of disgust arises from the sympathetic, albeit perhaps sarcastic or ironic, description of the situation of "गावठी पोलार्ड" (gāvaṭhī polārḍa), which implies something low-quality or undesirable facing a bad outcome. The phrase "बिचारा" (bichārā), meaning "poor guy," further reinforces the negative sentiment and contributes to the disgust by highlighting the unfortunate circumstances.

Text: तुला हे बोलण्याचा अधिकार तरी आहे का?रक्षक बन गया भक्षक.
translated_text: Do you have the right to say this? The protector has become the predator.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "रक्षक बन गया भक्षक" (rakshak ban gaya bhakshak - protector has become the predator). This indicates betrayal and a negative transformation, signifying a decline in moral standing.

Text: शेटजींनी बोलावे आणि बुद्धी गहाण टाकलेल्यानी मान डोलवावी.
translated_text: Shetji should speak and those who have lost their minds should bow their heads.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust emotion are driven by the phrase "बुद्धी गहाण टाकलेल्यानी" (buddhi gahan taklelyanini), which translates to "those who have lost their minds," implying a strong sense of contempt and disapproval.

Text: ह्या असल्या गिधाडा कडून कसली अपेक्षा करताय.... नीचं पातळीचे लोक आहेत हे...मूर्ख
translated_text: What do you expect from such vultures.... These are low-level people...fools
Justification: The phrase "गिधाडा कडून" (gidhadakadoon - from vultures) is a derogatory term, and the words "नीचं पातळीचे" (neech pathaliche - low-level) and "मूर्ख" (murkh - fools) are insults, collectively indicating strong negative sentiment and disgust.

Text: मग वकील पण काय आता भाजपानेच द्यायचा का चांगला
translated_text: So why should BJP provide lawyers now?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the sarcastic tone implied by "काय आता भाजपानेच द्यायचा का चांगला" (kay aata BJPnech dyaycha ka changla), questioning if BJP is solely responsible for providing lawyers, suggesting a negative view towards BJP's involvement or responsibility.

Text:
उगी उगी.. 

आपण त्यांचं नाव मॅडमला सांगू.. मग बसा म्हणावं बोंबलत...
translated_text:
Ugi ugi.. 

Let's tell his name to Madam.. Then sit down and say Bombalat...
Justification: The negative sentiment is primarily driven by the phrase "बोंबलत" (Bombalat), which translates to "screaming" or "yelling" and implies a negative consequence or punishment. The sentence suggests a threat and schadenfreude.

Text: सुशांतसिंग राजपूत आणि दिशा सलियान यांचाही आत्मा न्यायासाठी तळमळत आहे !!
translated_text: The souls of Sushant Singh Rajput and Disha Salian are also yearning for justice!!
Justification: The phrase "न्यायासाठी तळमळत आहे !!" (nyayasathi talmalat aahe!!) meaning "yearning for justice!!" expresses a strong desire for justice that remains unfulfilled. This yearning, combined with the context of death and a perceived lack of resolution, evokes feelings of anger and frustration, resulting in a negative sentiment.

Text: ए भवानि हे कोणा साठि आहे ते ही सांग।
translated_text: Oh Bhavani, tell me who this is.
Justification: The phrase "ए भवानि" (Oh Bhavani) expresses a sense of disrespect or impatience, contributing to the negative sentiment. While it's a call to a deity, the context suggests annoyance rather than reverence.

Text: आरोग्यमंत्र्यांच तोंड आहे की गटार??? म्हणे आताच मराठ्यांना आरक्षणाची खाज का??
translated_text: Is the Health Minister's mouth or a sewer??? Why are Marathas itching for reservation now??
Justification: The phrase "तोंड आहे की गटार???" ("mouth or a sewer???") is highly offensive and conveys strong negativity and disgust, thus indicating a negative sentiment.

Text: श्रद्धेवरील पुरोगाम्यांची टीका आणि खंडण !
translated_text: Progressives' criticism and refutation of faith!
Justification: "टीका आणि खंडण !" (criticism and refutation!) expresses strong negativity and disapproval, indicating anger.

Text: ... कोणती कुठंही  पोरगा काय नगरसेवक होतं नाही.. चिमण्या
translated_text: ... No boy became a corporator.. Chimanya
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is primarily driven by the phrase "कोणती कुठंही ... होतं नाही" (konta kuthehi... hota nahi) which implies a complete lack of possibility or denial, conveying a negative assertion. While "चिमण्या" (Chimanya) is present, its role is less significant in determining the overall sentiment in this context.

Text: या महिला काय स्पेशल आहे का अट शिथिल करायला?
translated_text: What is special about this woman that the condition should be relaxed?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "काय स्पेशल आहे का" (kay special aahe ka), which implies skepticism and challenges the justification for relaxing the rules, reflecting a negative connotation of questioning special treatment.

Text: बरोबर आहे मुलाखती पासून अंधभक्त नाना वर खूपच भुंकत आहेत
translated_text: That's right, the blind devotees have been barking a lot at Nana since the interview.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the word "भुंकत" (bhunkat), meaning "barking," which is used figuratively to describe the blind devotees' behavior towards Nana, implying a disrespectful and aggressive attitude.

Text: इथे बरा हो बोलशील आणि तिथे उमेदवार पण तुम्हीच देणार पोटनिवडणुकीला....हरामखोर....
translated_text: You can speak well here and you will be the one to give the candidate for the by-election there....scoundrel....
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the use of the word "हरामखोर" (haraamkhor), which translates to "scoundrel," a derogatory term.

Text: हे संघवाले , गाईच्या मुत्रचे भक्त.
translated_text: These Sangh members are devotees of cow urine.
Justification: "गाईच्या मुत्रचे भक्त" (devotees of cow urine) expresses disgust due to the perceived absurdity and lack of hygiene associated with the practice, leading to a negative sentiment.

Text: आधी धनुष्यबाण गेलं, आता मशाल ही गेली
translated_text: First the bow and arrow went, now the torch has gone.
Justification: The sentence expresses a loss of symbols, particularly political ones. The phrase "आधी...गेलं, आता...ही गेली" (first...gone, now...also gone) suggests a pattern of loss and therefore a negative sentiment.

Text: माझ्या संपर्कात पण   -   बाळासाहेब नावाचे लोक आहेत तेपण याचे बाप आहेत का?
translated_text: I am in contact with people named Balasaheb, is he also his father?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the rhetorical and sarcastic question "याचे बाप आहेत का?" (yache baap aahet ka?), implying doubt and negativity towards the subject.

Text: शिवसेना आहे ना. गद्दारांना दिसत नाहीये ती
translated_text: Shiv Sena is there, right? Traitors don't see it.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust are primarily driven by the word "गद्दारांना" (gaddaranna), meaning "traitors," which expresses strong condemnation and contempt.

Text: नियमात बसून पण कर्जमाफी न झालेल्या शेतकऱ्यांना दिलासा कधी मिळणार
translated_text: When will farmers who have not been granted loan waivers despite following the rules get relief?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "कर्जमाफी न झालेल्या" (karjamafi na zalyelya), meaning "who have not been granted loan waivers," highlighting the negative situation of farmers not receiving the expected benefit.

Text: तुझ्यासारखंच अण्णाजी ने तिला पण सोडल आहे भुंकायला
translated_text: Just like you, Annaji has also left her to bark.
Justification: The phrase "भुंकायला" (bhunkayla - to bark) implies a dehumanizing comparison, suggesting the person is being treated like an animal. This strongly contributes to the negative sentiment.

Text: स्वतःला शुद्र शुद्र म्हणून करोडपती झाला हा
translated_text: He became a millionaire by calling himself a Shudra.
Justification: The negative sentiment is primarily driven by the phrase "शुद्र शुद्र" (Shudra Shudra) which is perceived negatively due to its historical association with caste-based discrimination and societal hierarchy. The repetition emphasizes the self-degradation used for financial gain, fueling the negative sentiment.

Text: हा माणूस मुख्यमंत्री झाला आणि जणू महाराष्ट्राला पनवतीच लागली
translated_text: This man became the Chief Minister and it was as if Maharashtra was in a state of flux.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "पनवतीच लागली" (panavatich lagli), which implies bad luck or misfortune has befallen Maharashtra since the man became Chief Minister.

Text: आतापर्यंत झोपला होता का
translated_text: Have you slept yet?
Justification: The sentence implies mild disapproval or surprise at the recipient having potentially slept for an extended period, indicated by the phrase "आतापर्यंत झोपला होता का" which translates to "Have you been sleeping until now?". This leads to a slightly negative sentiment.

Text: श्रीलंकेचा प्रमूख वेगवान गोलंदाज दुशमंता चमिरा दुखापतीमुळे टी   विश्वचषक स्पर्धेतून बाहेर झाला आहे.
translated_text: Sri Lanka's leading fast bowler Dushmantha Chameera has been ruled out of the T20 World Cup due to injury.
Justification: The phrase "दुखापतीमुळे टी विश्वचषक स्पर्धेतून बाहेर झाला आहे" (dukhaapatimule T vishwachasak spardhetun baahar jhala aahe) indicating that he is out of the T20 World Cup due to injury, evokes a negative sentiment as it represents a loss or setback.

Text: आता दिवाळी सुद्धा आंधारातल्या कोठडीत..
translated_text: Now even Diwali is in a dark cell..
Justification: The sentence expresses negative sentiment because "आंधारातल्या कोठडीत" (aandharaatlya kothdit), meaning "in a dark cell," evokes a feeling of confinement and bleakness, specifically associating Diwali with a negative situation.

Text: वाद कसा होऊ शकेल उद्धव साहेबांना सोफ्टी आईस्क्रिम दिले आहे त्यांना मशाल वाटले
translated_text: How can there be a dispute? Soft ice cream was given to Uddhav Saheb and he felt like a torch.
Justification: The phrase "मशाल वाटले" (mashaal vatale), meaning "felt like a torch" (implying something fiery and potentially destructive/powerful was felt instead of something pleasant like ice cream), indicates anger and dissatisfaction, leading to a negative sentiment.

Text: निकाल सप्टेंबर महिन्यात लावणार होते... माननीय आयोग ऑक्टोंबर मध्ये अंतिम उत्तरतालिका देत आहे.
translated_text: The results were supposed to be declared in September... The Honorable Commission is releasing the final answer key in October.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "लावणार होते" (lavnar hote - were supposed to), indicating a delay from the initial plan. While the information about the answer key might bring some relief, it doesn't outweigh the disappointment of the delayed results, therefore the overall sentiment is negative. The joy emotion likely stems from the fact that at least the answer key is being released providing partial relief/progress in the matter.

Text: सरंजामशाही व घराणेशाही चे उत्तम उदाहरण. ठाकरे शिवाय बाकी सर्व चपलेची धुळ
translated_text: A perfect example of feudalism and dynastic rule. Except Thackeray, everyone else is a piece of cake.
Justification: "चपलेची धुळ" (chappalechi dhul - dust of a slipper/shoe) is a derogatory phrase implying worthlessness, thus contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: तुम्ही फक्त मुस्लिमांचे लांगुलचालन करा,बाकी महागाई वाढली ती कमी करायचं काम मोदी करतील.
translated_text: You just support Muslims, Modi will do the rest to reduce inflation.
Justification: The phrase "फक्त मुस्लिमांचे लांगुलचालन करा" (fakt muslimanche langulchalan kara), meaning "you just support Muslims," expresses a negative sentiment due to its potentially divisive and accusatory tone, implying favoritism towards a specific group.

Text: हि डब्बा संघटना फ्रॉड आहे तुमच्यासारखी
translated_text: This Dabba organization is a fraud like you.
Justification: "फ्रॉड आहे तुमच्यासारखी" (fraud aahe tumchyasarkhi) is the key phrase expressing negativity and anger because it directly accuses the listener/target of being like a fraudulent organization, indicating strong disapproval and animosity.

Text: जनतेने विरोधी पक्षनेते पद देखील मिळणार नाही आशी काँग्रेसची लायकी ठेवलिय
translated_text: The people have made the Congress worthy of not even getting the post of Leader of the Opposition.
Justification: The negative sentiment and anger emotion are driven by the phrase "लायकी ठेवलिय" (layaki thevliya), which implies that the Congress party is deemed unworthy and incompetent, leading to a strongly negative assessment.

Text: जशी तुमच्या डोक्यात गेली बर झाल हे तुम्हीच मान्य केल खाजप महाराष्ट्र द्रोही
translated_text: You have to accept it as it is in your head, Khajap Maharashtra traitor.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "महाराष्ट्र द्रोही" (Maharashtra traitor), which is a highly accusatory and negative term.

Text: राठोड चा मांडीला मंडी लावून तुम्ही बसणार आणि दुसऱ्याला अक्कल शिकवणार का।काकू
translated_text: Will you sit on Rathod's lap and teach common sense to others? Aunty
Justification: The phrase "मांडीला मंडी लावून" (mandila mandi lavun), meaning "sitting on someone's lap" (specifically Rathod's lap in this context), implies inappropriate intimacy and questionable moral standing, contributing to the negative sentiment. It suggests hypocrisy when coupled with "अक्कल शिकवणार" (akkal shikavnar), meaning "teaching common sense," implying the speaker lacks the authority or credibility to advise others.

Text: अशी अफवा पसरली आहे की, काकू सुध्दा सुरत ला जाणार आहेत..
translated_text: Rumor has it that Aunty is also going to Surat.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the word "अफवा" (Afva), which translates to "rumor". Rumors inherently carry a sense of uncertainty and potential negativity, hence the negative sentiment.

Text: जास्त रडू नका आता बस करा.... दररोज उठून रडत बसायचं सोडून द्या
translated_text: Don't cry too much, just sit down now.... Stop waking up and crying every day.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "जास्त रडू नका" (jasta radu naka), meaning "don't cry too much," and the repeated emphasis on crying with "दररोज उठून रडत बसायचं" (darroj uthun radat basaych), implying habitual sadness.

Text: हा हासून रायला का रडून राहिला बे
translated_text: Why did Raya cry after laughing?
Justification: "का रडून राहिला" (ka radun rahila - why cry) indicates a negative sentiment, contrasting with the initial laughter and expressing surprise at the shift in emotion.

Text: जगातील सर्वात रिकामचोट जमात म्हणजे शिल्लक शेणिक
translated_text: The most empty tribe in the world is the remaining Sheniks.
Justification: "रिकामचोट" (rikaamchot), meaning empty or useless, carries a strongly negative connotation and contributes to the negative sentiment. This word expresses a derogatory and insulting judgment, leading to sadness.

Text: सकाळी आंदोलन आणि रात्री वेश बदलून जाऊद्या टिपा द्याला
translated_text: Advise them to protest in the morning and change their clothes at night.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "वेश बदलून जाऊद्या टिपा द्याला" (vesh badlun jau dya tipa dyala), which implies deception and potentially malicious intent, suggesting they are changing clothes to give tips, hinting at trickery. While "आंदोलन" (andolan - protest) itself can be neutral depending on context, combined with the deception, it contributes to the negative sentiment.

Text: मुलगा विश्वासघातकी, ऐतखाऊ, खुनशी, घमंडी व आळशी कर्तृत्वशून्य आहे. फुकटचंबू बाबूराव!
translated_text: The son is treacherous, greedy, murderous, arrogant, lazy and incompetent. Free-spirited Baburao!
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the cluster of derogatory terms used to describe the son, specifically "विश्वासघातकी, ऐतखाऊ, खुनशी, घमंडी व आळशी," which translates to treacherous, greedy, murderous, arrogant, and lazy, indicating strong disapproval.

Text: नाना ईतका ढुंगणात जोर आहे तर राहुल गांधी ला महाराष्ट्रात निवडून आणून दाखव की
translated_text: If there is so much power in the ass, then show it by getting Rahul Gandhi elected in Maharashtra.
Justification: The negative sentiment is primarily driven by the phrase "ढुंगणात जोर आहे" (dhungnaat jor aahe), which literally translates to "power in the ass." This is a vulgar and disrespectful expression, conveying strong disapproval and negativity.

Text: काहीही बोलतो का रे
translated_text: Are you saying anything?
Justification: The phrase "काहीही बोलतो का रे" implies disbelief and criticism due to the "काहीही" (anything) and the informal, slightly dismissive "रे," suggesting the speaker finds the statement questionable or nonsensical, leading to a negative sentiment.

Text: माझ्यावर टीका करण्याचं कंत्राट काँग्रेसने दुसऱ्याला दिलं आहे – पंतप्रधान मोदींचं विधान!
translated_text: Congress has given the contract to criticize me to someone else – Prime Minister Modi's statement!
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "टीका करण्याचं कंत्राट" (ṭīkā karaṇyāchẽ kaṁṭrāṭ), which translates to "contract to criticize." This suggests a deliberate and malicious effort to undermine the speaker, fueling anger.

Text: तुम्हाला महाराष्ट्रातील जनता एकाच नावाने ओळखते ते नाव म्हणजे
translated_text: The people of Maharashtra know you by one name, that name is
Justification: The sentence introduces a statement but lacks emotional indicators. While setting the stage for something, it doesn't contain any negative sentiment. Therefore, the sentiment is neutral. There isn't a specific Marathi word or phrase responsible for a negative sentiment.

Text: त्यांच्या आवाक्याबाहेर आहे, म्हणूनच हे धार्मिक मुद्दे सामोर करतात।
translated_text: It is beyond their reach, which is why they confront these religious issues.
Justification: The sentence expresses a negative sentiment due to the phrase "आवाक्याबाहेर आहे" (aavakyabaher aahe), meaning "beyond their reach." This indicates a limitation or inability, contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: मराठमोळी लटके ताई तिला जो त्रास देताय हे जग विसरणार नाही
translated_text: The world will not forget the trouble that Latke Tai is giving her, a Marathi woman.
Justification: "त्रास देताय" (tras detaya), meaning "giving trouble," indicates a negative sentiment as it describes an action causing distress.

Text: यशस्वी माघारची दिली का?
translated_text: Did you make a successful withdrawal?
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the implication that the withdrawal may not have been "successful" (यशस्वी), suggesting a potential problem or failure.

Text: हे म्हणजे, लग्न दुसऱ्याचं अन् नाचतय येड्या भो*च!
translated_text: This means, someone else's wedding and you're dancing, you idiot!
Justification: The phrase "येड्या भो*च" (yedya bho*ch), a highly offensive and derogatory term, is a clear indicator of anger and a negative sentiment.

Text: सगळे मिळून येड्यात काढत आहेत जनतेला
translated_text: Everyone is pulling the public together.
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is driven by the phrase "येड्यात काढत आहेत" (yedyaat kadhat aahet), which means "fooling" or "making a fool of," indicating a negative action being done to the public.

Text: आधीपासूनच रडके आहेत ही लोकं
translated_text: These people are already crying.
Justification: "रडके" (radke), meaning "crying" or "whiny," indicates a negative characteristic, contributing to the negative sentiment. The emotion is neutral because the sentence simply states a fact about the people, without expressing strong feeling or judgment beyond the inherent negativity of being "crying".

Text: दादा यांना पोकळ बांबु चा प्रसाद दिल्याशिवाय हे लोकं सरळ होणार नाहीत..
translated_text: These people will not become righteous unless they give Dada a hollow bamboo offering.
Justification: The sentence expresses negative sentiment and disgust due to the phrase "पोकळ बांबु चा प्रसाद" (pokal bambu cha prasad - hollow bamboo offering), which implies a mocking or belittling form of punishment or treatment, evoking feelings of disgust and disapproval.

Text: उद्धव साहेबंनी तुम्हाला लायकि पेक्षा भरपुर दिले होते म्हणुन तुम्ही गद्दारी केलीत काय
translated_text: Did you betray Uddhav Saheb because he gave you more than you deserved?
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the word "गद्दारी" (gaddari), meaning betrayal.

Text: बाळासाहेब तर तुमच्या बापाला ठणाणा शिव्या द्यायचे हा बहुधा प्रेमळ शिव्या असतील त्या
translated_text: Balasaheb, if you were to give your father a harsh scolding, it would probably be a loving scolding.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "ठणाणा शिव्या" (thanana shivya), which translates to "harsh scolding" or "abusive language," even with the qualifier "प्रेमळ" (premal - loving) it doesn't negate the initial negative impression.

Text: मग ते दहशतवादी मुस्लिम तुमच्या मध्ये काय करत होते????
translated_text: So what were those terrorist Muslims doing among you????
Justification: The phrase "दहशतवादी मुस्लिम" (dahashatwadi Muslim) meaning "terrorist Muslim" carries a strong negative connotation, leading to the negative sentiment. The surprise element comes from the question implying disbelief or shock that such individuals were present "तुमच्या मध्ये" (tumchya madhye) - "among you".

Text: बायकांनी मनाची तरी लाज ठेवावी...कूंक हे कपाळावर गोल लागतं.. उभं नाही..
translated_text: Women should at least be ashamed of their hearts...this is a round mark on the forehead.. not a standing one..
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "मनाची तरी लाज ठेवावी...", which expresses disapproval and disgust, implying women should be ashamed of their behavior.

Text: आईच्या दूधात आढळलं ‘मायक्रोप्लास्टिक’, धक्कादायक माहिती समोर
translated_text: Microplastic' found in mother's milk, shocking information revealed
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "धक्कादायक" (dhakkadayak), which translates to "shocking" in English, indicating a negative and alarming discovery.

Text: टच केले तर राजीनामा देणार होता गद्दार.... आता काय?
translated_text: The traitor was going to resign if he was touched....what now?
Justification: The word "गद्दार" (gaddar), meaning traitor, indicates a negative sentiment and elicits anger.

Text: अतुल तुझ्याकडे या भुंकणाऱ्या बघून कुत्र्यांची आठवण होते
translated_text: Atul, looking at you, these barking dogs remind me of dogs.
Justification: The phrase "भुंकणाऱ्या बघून कुत्र्यांची" (bhunkarnarya baghun kutryanchi - barking dogs) carries a negative connotation, suggesting a comparison to something unpleasant or annoying. This results in a negative sentiment.

Text: पप्पू ची गोटी आहे ही
translated_text: This is Pappu's hat.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "पप्पू ची गोटी आहे ही" ("Pappu chi goti aahe hi"). While literally meaning "This is Pappu's hat," it's used idiomatically to imply that someone or something is simple, foolish, or easily manipulated, thus conveying a negative connotation despite the literal meaning.

Text: महागाई वाघावाण्याचे तंत्र आहे हे. बघा पूर्वीचेच दर आणतात ते काय? आणणारच नाहीत.
translated_text: This is a technique to control inflation. Look, what are they bringing in the same prices as before? They won't bring them in.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from "आणणारच नाहीत" (aananarach nahit), which translates to "they won't bring them in." This phrase indicates disbelief and a negative expectation that prices will not return to previous levels, contributing to the negative sentiment. The surprise likely comes from the ironic comparison of the high prices to an inflation-controlling technique.

Text: हा माणूसच आहे त्याचे ट्विट काय घेऊन बसलात
translated_text: This is the man, what are you doing with his tweets?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the dismissive and belittling tone conveyed by "काय घेऊन बसलात" (kay gheun baslaat), which implies that the tweets are insignificant and not worth paying attention to, thus showing disdain.

Text: याला ब्रह्मास्त्र नाही खालच्या पातळीचे राजकारण बोलतात
translated_text: This guy doesn't have a Brahmastra, he talks about low level politics.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust emotion are driven by the phrase "खालच्या पातळीचे राजकारण" (khalchya pataliche rajkaran), meaning "low level politics," which expresses disapproval and contempt for the described behavior.

Text: अवघड जागी दुखन आणि जावयी डॉक्टर अशी परिस्थिती झालिये अंध भक्तांची
translated_text: Blind devotees are in a difficult situation, suffering and having to face a doctor.
Justification: "अवघड" (awkward/difficult) indicates a negative situation, contributing to the negative sentiment. The overall context further reinforces this.

Text: आपण गद्दार विचारश्रेणीतुन बोलताय हे न समजण्या येवढा दुधखुळा आत्ता महाराष्ट्र राहीला नाही
translated_text: There is no one left in Maharashtra who is so ignorant that they don't understand that we are speaking from a traitorous ideology.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the Marathi word "गद्दार" (gaddar), meaning "traitor," which carries a strong negative connotation.

Text: नाही मराठी कुत्रकारांना मात्र पाकीट म्हाडा फ्लॅट निश्चित देणार
translated_text: No, Marathi dog owners will definitely be given a wallet and a MHADA flat.
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is primarily driven by the initial word "नाही" (nahi), meaning "no". This explicitly negates a positive outcome or statement, immediately setting a negative tone. While the rest of the sentence offers potential benefits, the initial rejection carries the most weight.

Text: "मीडियाकडून अभिव्यक्ती स्वातंत्र्याच्या नावाखाली फूट पाडण्याचा प्रयत्न होत आहे." -संरक्षणमंत्री राजनाथ सिंह
translated_text: "Media is trying to create division in the name of freedom of expression." -Defense Minister Rajnath Singh
Justification: The phrase "फूट पाडण्याचा प्रयत्न" (phoot paadnyacha prayatna), meaning "trying to create division," indicates a negative sentiment and triggers anger due to its suggestion of malicious intent.

Text: सरकार तुपाशी आणि जनता उपाशी... जय महाराष्ट्र
translated_text: The government is with you and the people are hungry... Hail Maharashtra
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "जनता उपाशी" (janta upashi), meaning "the people are hungry," which highlights suffering and disparity. Although "जय महाराष्ट्र" (Jai Maharashtra) expresses joy/pride, the preceding statement outweighs it.

Text: अंध भंक्तांसाठी भिक मागायची व्यवस्था करण्यात आली आहे
translated_text: Arrangements have been made for blind devotees to beg.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "भिक मागायची" (bhik magayachi), meaning "to beg," which implies a demeaning and undesirable situation for the "अंध भंक्तांसाठी" (andha bhaktansathi), or "blind devotees." The act of begging carries negative connotations, leading to the overall negative sentiment.

Text: माझ्या समोर असे घडत असते तर धुनाई केली असती. एखाद्याला तरी चांगलच ठोकल असत.
translated_text: If this had happened in front of me, I would have been furious. I would have beaten someone up real good.
Justification: The negative sentiment and anger are primarily driven by the phrases "धुनाई केली असती" (dhunai keli asti - would have thrashed) and "चांगलच ठोकल असत" (changlach thokla asta - would have beaten up real good) which express violent intentions and strong negative feelings.

Text: त्यांना आपले महत्त्व माहिती नाही अजून बहुतेक
translated_text: They don't know their importance yet, most of them.
Justification: The sentence has a negative sentiment due to the phrase "माहिती नाही" (mahitI nAhI), which translates to "don't know," indicating a lack of awareness that reflects a deficiency. The overall sentiment is negative although it does not trigger a strong emotion.

Text: जुलाब झालेल्या वेक्तीला पण जग पिवळं पिवळं दिसते
translated_text: Even a person with diarrhea sees the world as yellow.
Justification: The sentence receives a negative sentiment because of the implication of sickness and discomfort associated with "जुलाब" (diarrhea). While the sentence itself is observational, the underlying condition is negative. The emotion is neutral because the sentence is stated factually without conveying sadness, anger or other specific emotions.

Text: साहेबांबद्दल असले तर्क लावताना तुमची जीभ कशी जड पडली नाही..
translated_text: How come your tongue didn't get heavy while making such arguments about Saheb?
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the rhetorical question "जीभ कशी जड पडली नाही" (jeebh kashi jad padli nahi), which implies disbelief and criticism towards the person making arguments about Saheb, suggesting the speaker finds the arguments disrespectful or inappropriate. The surprise emotion arises from the incredulity expressed in the question.

Text: तुझ्या बापाच्या वयाचे आहेत ते, तू बापाशी असाच बोलतो का रे
translated_text: They're your father's age, do you talk to your father like that?
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) stems from the phrase "तू बापाशी असाच बोलतो का रे" (tu bapaashi asach bolto ka re), which questions the speaker's disrespectful tone towards someone of their father's age, implying disapproval and criticism.

Text: फक्त वसुली आणि लूट हाच हेतू आणि साध्य,समस्या सोडवणे हा हेतूच नाही.
translated_text: The only purpose and achievement is recovery and loot, not solving the problem.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "फक्त वसुली आणि लूट हाच हेतू" (phakt vasuli aani loot haach hetu) which means "the only purpose is recovery and loot," indicating a negative focus on exploitation rather than problem-solving.

Text: आता मनसे वाल्यांनी हळूहळू पहिल्या सारखं भाजपवर पण टीका करणं चालू केलं तरी चालेल.
translated_text: Now, it would be okay if MNS members gradually started criticizing BJP like before.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "टीका करणं चालू केलं तरी चालेल" (tika karan chalu kela tari chalel) which implies criticism resuming, indicating a potentially negative relationship or conflict.

Text: इथे कारवाई का होत नाही
translated_text: Why is no action being taken here?
Justification: The phrase "का होत नाही" (ka hot nahi - why is not happening) expresses frustration and questions the lack of action, leading to a negative sentiment and anger.

Text: चूक आहे... ही सहानुभूती नाही निष्ठेचा ज्वालामुखी आहे.
translated_text: That's wrong... This is not sympathy, it's a volcano of loyalty.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "चूक आहे" (chuk aahe), meaning "that's wrong," indicating disapproval. The disgust emotion is reinforced by the implied condemnation and contrast with something far more intense and potentially destructive ("निष्ठेचा ज्वालामुखी आहे" - a volcano of loyalty).

Text: रामदास रडला होता तेव्हाच झाली दादा लागू
translated_text: Dada Lagoo came into effect only when Ramdas was crying.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from "रडला होता" (radla hota), meaning "was crying," which indicates sadness and a generally undesirable situation.

Text: हा नाच्या वाकड्या पवारचा गुलाम आहे. डोक वाकड्याच अन नाच्या चे.
translated_text: This is a slave of the crooked Pawar. He has a crooked head and is a crooked man.
Justification: The negative sentiment is primarily driven by the phrase "वाकड्या पवारचा गुलाम" (vakdya pawarcha ghulam) and "नाच्या" (nachya). "गुलाम" (ghulam) means slave which inherently carries a negative connotation. "वाकड्या" (vakdya) meaning crooked, implies dishonesty and untrustworthiness, further amplifying the negative sentiment. "नाच्या" (nachya) is a derogatory term meaning crooked or twisted person further confirming the negative sentiment.

Text: त्यांनी बंडखोरी केल्याने ते त्यांना मिळेल असे वाटत नाही!
translated_text: They don't think they'll get it by rebelling!
Justification: The phrase "बंडखोरी केल्याने" (bandakhori kelyaane), meaning "by rebelling," implies a negative action that is unlikely to yield positive results, contributing to a negative sentiment and potential anger.

Text: डिलिट केले कारण ट्विट अगोदर दोन जणांनी टाकले होते लवडेश्र्वर
translated_text: Deleted because the tweet was previously posted by two people Lovedeshwar
Justification: The word "लवडेश्र्वर" (Lovedeshwar), while potentially a name, carries a negative connotation in this context, contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: कोथळा सम्राटला कधी शहानपन सुचणार काय माहित आयुष्यात कधी पत्र लिहीलय का किंबहुना सम्राटांनी
translated_text: I wonder if the Kothala Emperor will ever think of wisdom. Has he ever written a letter in his life? In fact, the Emperor
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "कधी शहानपन सुचणार काय माहित", which translates to "I wonder if he will ever think of wisdom," expressing doubt and a lack of expectation of positive change.

Text: भाषा ही फक्त भाषा असते ज्ञान नसतं रे गोट्या..
translated_text: Language is just language, not knowledge, you idiot.
Justification: The negative sentiment and anger are primarily driven by the dismissive and condescending phrase "रे गोट्या" (re gotya), which translates to "you idiot" or a similar derogatory term. This phrase diminishes the person being addressed and conveys strong disapproval.

Text: राजकारण्यांच्या फेकलेल्या तूकड्यांवर जगणारी जमात आहे ही
translated_text: This is a community that lives on the scraps thrown by politicians.
Justification: "फेकलेल्या तूकड्यांवर जगणारी" (living on thrown scraps) implies dependence and a lack of agency, indicating a negative sentiment.

Text: हा परीवार वाद नाही का,
translated_text: Isn't this a family dispute?
Justification: The negative sentiment and surprise emotion are driven by the phrase "वाद नाही का" (vaad nahi ka), which translates to "isn't it a dispute?". The interrogative and negative framing suggests a surprised and potentially disapproving tone towards what is perceived as a family dispute.

Text: हत्ती वर भुंकणाऱ्या कुत्र्यांना हत्ती भिक घालत नाही
translated_text: Elephants do not beg for dogs that bark at them.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "भिक घालत नाही" (bhik ghalat nahi), meaning "do not beg/care about," implying indifference and disregard, thus signaling a negative connotation towards the dogs.

Text: पालघर प्रकरण जाळून खाक व्हायची वेळ आली आहे.
translated_text: It is time for the Palghar case to burn to ashes.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "जाळून खाक व्हायची वेळ आली आहे" (jaLun khak vhaychi vel aali aahe), which translates to "it is time to burn to ashes," indicating a desire for complete destruction and expressing a strong negative feeling.

Text: का नाही? ते काय देव आहेत का ???
translated_text: Why not? Are they gods???
Justification: The sentence expresses negative sentiment due to the rhetorical question questioning someone's status "ते काय देव आहेत का ???" (te kay dev aahet ka??) implying they are not divine and thus are not exempt from criticism, expressing disbelief and borderline sarcasm. The phrase "देव आहेत का?? (dev aahet ka??)" directly contributes to the negative sentiment through this implication.

Text: लोकसभेला माढा मध्ये महाराष्ट्राच्या तथाकथित चाणक्य ची अशीच फाटली होती...
translated_text: This is how the so-called Chanakya of Maharashtra was torn apart in the Lok Sabha...
Justification: The phrase "फाटली होती" (phatli hoti), meaning "was torn apart," indicates a negative sentiment as it implies defeat or humiliation.

Text: खाल्लेल्या ताटात हगणारी ही लोक
translated_text: These people who eat in the plate
Justification: The phrase "हगणारी" (haganari), meaning "defecating," is highly derogatory and contributes to the negative sentiment.

Text: हा अंब्या सगळ्या जगाला ज्ञान देतो पण स्वता मात्र फाटका रहातो.
translated_text: This Ambya gives knowledge to the whole world but remains a gate unto himself.
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is driven by the phrase "फाटका रहातो" (phataka rahato), which translates to "remains a gate unto himself" but connotes being poor or unfulfilled despite benefiting others. This phrase implies a negative outcome for the subject despite positive actions.

Text: .  वर्ष गप्प का होते सांगणार का? सत्ता गेल्यावर कसे जागे झाले?
translated_text: . Will you tell me why you were silent for a year? How did you wake up after losing power?
Justification: The phrase "सत्ता गेल्यावर कसे जागे झाले?" (How did you wake up after losing power?) implies criticism and disbelief, suggesting a negative sentiment. The surprise emotion is evoked by the questioning of their sudden change in behavior.

Text: संजय राऊत सारखा चुत्या आणी बिनडोक व्यक्ती पुन्हा होणें नाही.!
translated_text: There will be no more stupid and mindless people like Sanjay Raut!
Justification: The sentiment is negative and evokes anger due to the use of the derogatory and abusive word "चुत्या" (chutya), directly insulting Sanjay Raut. The phrase "बिनडोक" (bindok), meaning mindless, further contributes to the negative and angry sentiment.

Text: कोणाला जाऊन चिकटले शेनके, लाल सलाम म्हणून विचारलं.
translated_text: I asked who went and stuck the stick, as a red salute.
Justification: The phrase "जाऊन चिकटले" (jaun chikatle - went and stuck) indicates a negative sentiment because it implies an unwelcome or forced association. While "लाल सलाम" (lal salaam - red salute) has political connotations, the primary negative sentiment arises from the "जाऊन चिकटले" part.

Text: लायकी आणि किंमत लाचार बनविते... याचे उत्तम उदाहरण।। शिव शिव।।
translated_text: Value and price make you helpless... A good example of this. Shiva Shiva.
Justification: The phrase "लाचार बनविते" (lachar banavite), meaning "make you helpless," indicates a negative sentiment due to its implication of a state of powerlessness.

Text: भल्या माणसा सुशांतसिंग केस वेळी झोपला होतास का
translated_text: Good man, were you sleeping during the Sushant Singh Rajput case?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the sarcastic use of "भल्या माणसा" (bhalya manasa - good man) which implies disapproval and questions the person's actions during a sensitive time.

Text: ढोंगी लोक आहेत..हे
translated_text: These people are hypocrites..
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust emotion are primarily driven by the word "ढोंगी" (ḍhōṅgī), which translates to "hypocrites". This word carries strong negative connotations, indicating disapproval and disgust.

Text: मग रात्री बेरात्री वेष बदलून काय गोठ्या खेळत होते का
translated_text: So, what were they playing with at night, changing their costumes?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the sarcastic tone implied by "काय गोठ्या खेळत होते का" (kay gothya khelat hote ka), which suggests disbelief and ridicule. It translates to "what were they playing with at night, changing their costumes?", implying that they were obviously doing something suspicious and not simply playing.

Text: हीच्या आईला लावा रे  कोणी तरी.. पूर्ण दिवसाची वाट लावली मेसेज करून
translated_text: Someone please get this mother.. I kept her waiting all day by sending messages.
Justification: The phrase "वाट लावली" expresses strong negativity and frustration, indicating anger. It literally translates to "spoiled" or "ruined," but in this context, it signifies extreme annoyance and anger at having one's day disrupted.

Text: भंपक नाना आहे सगळं शहाणपणा फक्त याच्या कडेच
translated_text: There are many wonders, but only this one has all the wisdom.
Justification: The sentence is negative due to the use of "भंपक" (bhampak), which implies nonsense or absurdity.

Text: नाचता येईना अंगण वाकडे अशी तुमची अवस्था आहे मोहोळ साहेब
translated_text: You are in such a state that you cannot dance and wander around the courtyard, Mohol Saheb.
Justification: "नाचता येईना अंगण वाकडे" is an idiom implying incompetence and blaming external factors, contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: शेतकऱ्याचे सगळं नुकसान झालेला आहे त्यामुळे ओला दुष्काळ जाहीर करावा
translated_text: Farmers have suffered losses, so a wet drought should be declared.
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is driven by the phrase "नुकसान झालेला आहे" (nuksan zalele aahe), meaning "losses have occurred/suffered losses".

Text: नाना पाटेकर यांच्याकडून आजच्या पिढीने आदर्श घ्यावा असे काहीही नाही.
translated_text: There is nothing that today's generation can take as an example from Nana Patekar.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "असे काहीही नाही" (ase kahihi nahi), which translates to "nothing like that" or "nothing at all," indicating a complete lack of positive attributes or examples to be taken from Nana Patekar.

Text: भाजपा म्हणजे भाजकी,जळकी पार्टी त्यामुळे ते असेच जळके लिहिणार,जैसी जीसकी सोच
translated_text: BJP is a party of BJP, a party of hatred, so they will write like that, just like the one who thinks like that.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrases "भाजकी,जळकी पार्टी" (bhajki, jalki party), which translates to "burnt/hated party," expressing strong disapproval.

Text: चा गॅस तीन पट झालाय हे का सांगत नाही भेकडांनो
translated_text: Why don't you tell me that gas has tripled, you cowards?
Justification: The phrase "भेकडांनो" (bhekadaanno), meaning "you cowards," is a derogatory term expressing contempt and anger, directly contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: गद्दार नाव हे खुप छान शोभतं नावापुढे चिंधे गटाला
translated_text: The name traitor suits the ragtag group very well.
Justification: "गद्दार" (gaddar), meaning "traitor," is a strongly negative term that directly contributes to the negative sentiment.

Text: बापाला पण असच बोलतो का बाळा
translated_text: Do you talk to your father like that too, baby?
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is driven by the rhetorical question and implied disapproval in the phrase "असच बोलतो का" (asach bolto ka), which questions the speaker's disrespectful behavior.

Text: -----आणि सूक्ष्मदर्शकाच्या सहाय्याने पण पाहाता येत नाही.
translated_text: -----And it cannot be seen even with a microscope.
Justification: The negative sentiment is derived from the phrase "येत नाही" (yet nahi), meaning "cannot be" or "is not possible," indicating a lack of ability and hence a negative connotation.

Text: तुमची भुमीका      ला समजली. कशाला उगाच असे करुन स्वतःच हास्य करुन घ्यायचं.
translated_text: I understand your point. Why would you do something like this and make yourself laugh?
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "उगाच असे करुन स्वतःच हास्य करुन घ्यायचं", which implies the speaker believes the listener's actions are pointless and self-deprecating, causing frustration and anger.

Text: आधिच भक्त च्या बुडाला काजळी आली
translated_text: The devotee's head was almost covered in soot.
Justification: "बुडाला काजळी आली" (covered in soot) implies a negative state or misfortune, contributing to the negative sentiment. While the emotion is neutral, the phrase itself denotes an undesirable circumstance.

Text: डोक्यात नाही बळ ,पन छातीत सदा कळ. असा हा कुजबळ..!
translated_text: There is no strength in the head, but always a struggle in the chest. Such is this madness..!
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the word "कुजबळ" (kujabal), which translates to "madness" and conveys a sense of disgust and negativity.

Text: भारत जिंकला ना,मग होताच तो नो बॉल
translated_text: India won, right? Then it was a no ball.
Justification: The phrase "मग होताच तो नो बॉल" (mag hotach to no ball), which translates to "Then it was a no ball anyway/obviously," implies a sense of disagreement or skepticism about the fairness of the victory due to the controversial no-ball decision, thus contributing to the negative sentiment despite the neutral emotion.

Text: सच्या तुझ्या बुडाला आग लागली म्हणजे तुला महाराज किती समजले हे समजलं की... भाकऱ्या
translated_text: Honestly, your belly caught fire, so I realized how much you understood Maharaj...
Justification: "बुडाला आग लागली" (belly caught fire) is a harsh, albeit figurative, expression implying misfortune and suffering, contributing to the negative sentiment. While the overall tone is sarcastic ("सच्या" - honestly and the implied lack of understanding), the core negativity stems from wishing misfortune, even metaphorically.

Text: मी स्वतः मेकॅनिकल इंजिनिअर आहे भाऊ आणि आपल्याला काही असते हा आपला गैरसमज आहे
translated_text: I am a mechanical engineer myself, bro, and it is your misconception that we have something.
Justification: The phrase "गैरसमज आहे" (gairasamaj ahe), meaning "is a misconception," indicates a negative sentiment as it implies disagreement or invalidation of someone else's belief.

Text: हा घ्या तपशील आणि करा भुंकायची सुरवात
translated_text: Take these details and start barking.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "भुंकायची सुरवात" (bhunkyachi survat), which translates to "start barking." This phrase is demeaning and insulting, implying that the recipient will engage in pointless or aggressive ranting.

Text: हा वेडा आहे सगळे बिनधास्त होते याचं सरकार असताना .
translated_text: This is crazy. Everyone was uninvolved when this government was in power.
Justification: The negative sentiment is primarily driven by the Marathi word "वेडा" (veda), meaning "crazy" or "mad," which expresses a negative judgment about the situation. The emotion of surprise arises from the unexpected or shocking nature of the statement.

Text: लायकी किती आणि बोलतो किती.. कशाला फालतू चे ट्विट करून स्वतःची इज्जत घालवतो...
translated_text: How much is he capable of and how much he talks.. Why waste his own dignity by tweeting nonsense...
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "फालतू चे" (faltu che) which translates to "nonsense" or "useless", expressing disgust for the content being created.

Text: याला उगी तुम्ही केला हे तर पदराखालच शेम्बडं निघाल
translated_text: If you did this to him, he would be a disgrace.
Justification: The phrase "पदराखालच शेम्बडं निघाल" (padrakhalach shembad nikal) which translates to "a disgrace" indicates a strong sense of disgust, thus justifying the negative sentiment and disgust emotion.

Text: च्यायला यांच्या...यातसुद्धा लबाडी....गुजराती म्हणजे जागतिक दर्जाचे लबाड आणि बंडलबाज....
translated_text: Cheyala's...this too is a lie....Gujaratis are world-class liars and troublemakers....
Justification: The phrase "लबाड आणि बंडलबाज" (liars and troublemakers) expresses a negative and derogatory sentiment, indicating disgust.

Text: अतिशय वाईट आहे हे सगळ! ह्यांना गावातल्या प्रथा...पूजा.. ह्यांचा ही आदर नाही?
translated_text: This is all very bad! Don't they have any respect for the village customs...worship...?
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "अतिशय वाईट आहे" (atishay wait aahe), which translates to "very bad." This directly expresses a negative evaluation of the situation, signaling a negative sentiment.

Text: अरे हा पण अर्धवट आणि याची माहिती पण अर्धवट.... खोटं बोल पण रेटुन बोल
translated_text: Oh, this is also half-baked and the information about this is also half-baked.... Tell a lie, but tell it quickly.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust are driven by the phrase "अर्धवट" (ardhvat - half-baked), implying incompleteness and inadequacy, compounded by the encouragement to lie ("खोटं बोल" - khot bol).

Text: पांढर्या कपड्यांची वाट लावायला आला हा पाऊस..
translated_text: This rain has come to make way for white clothes..
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to "वाट लावायला" (vaat lavayala), which implies spoiling or ruining something.

Text: आज दारि्द्र वाढवणारा दिवस साजरा केला तर चालेल का.? सर्व राजकारणी चोर आहेत.
translated_text: Would it be okay to celebrate a day that increases poverty today? All politicians are thieves.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "सर्व राजकारणी चोर आहेत" (sarva rajkarani chor aahet), meaning "all politicians are thieves," which expresses a negative opinion.

Text: तुम्हाला नीच राजकारणी असं म्हणायचं आहे का?
translated_text: Do you mean a lowly politician?
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the word "नीच" (neech), which translates to "lowly" or "despicable" in English, implying a negative assessment of the politician's character.

Text: भविष्यात हा व्यक्ती पुण्यात मोदीजींचे मोठ्ठाले बॅनर लावण्यापुरताच उरेल..!!
translated_text: In the future, this person will only be left to put up big banners of Modiji in Pune..!!
Justification: The negative sentiment and anger are driven by the phrase "मोठ्ठाले बॅनर लावण्यापुरताच उरेल" (moththale banner lavnyapuratach urel), which implies that the person will be reduced to a menial and insignificant task, showing contempt and belittling them.

Text: हा कोलतोय ते बघा.. ह्यांना काही बोलणार कि सेटिंग झालेय??
translated_text: Look at this Koltoy.. Are you going to tell them something or is it set up??
Justification: The negative sentiment arises from the sarcastic implication of "सेटिंग झालेय" (setting zale ahe), suggesting a corrupt arrangement or bias, leading to surprise at the implied unfairness.

Text: कविता वाचून मतदान होत नाहीं रे बाबा
translated_text: You don't vote by reading poetry, Dad.
Justification: The phrase "नाहीं रे बाबा" (naahi re baba) expresses a dismissive tone, contributing to the negative sentiment (-1). While "बाबा" means "Dad," in this context, it's used with "नाहीं रे" to indicate disbelief or disapproval.

Text: कठीण आहे
translated_text: It is difficult.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the word "कठीण" (kathin), which translates to "difficult," implying hardship and contributing to a feeling of sadness.

Text: सावंत साहेब हे बोलणे तुम्हाला शोभते का..?
translated_text: Mr. Sawant, does this sound good to you?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "शोभते का..?", which implies disapproval or that the statement is unbecoming of Mr. Sawant. The phrase questions the appropriateness of his words, thus creating a negative tone.

Text: हा येडझवा तिवारी
translated_text: This is Yadzwa Tiwari.
Justification: The word "येडझवा" (yadzava) is a highly offensive and derogatory slur, contributing to the negative sentiment. While the emotion is assessed as neutral, the inherent negativity of the word is undeniable.

Text: आले होते सहाव्या मजल्यावर निवद ठेवला होता पण ते पहिल्या मजल्यावर घुटमळत होते
translated_text: They had arrived and were staying on the sixth floor, but they were crouching on the first floor.
Justification: "घुटमळत होते" (ghutmalat hote), meaning "crouching/hesitating," suggests uncertainty or reluctance, contributing to a negative sentiment.

Text: मशाल समुद्रात बुडवणार होता हा कुले...
translated_text: This clan was about to throw the torch into the sea...
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is primarily driven by the phrase "मशाल समुद्रात बुडवणार होता" (mashaal samudraat budavanaar hota), meaning "was about to throw the torch into the sea," implying an act of sabotage or destruction.

Text: हा आशिष शेलार दरिद्री निघाला रे देवा.... काय काय करू लागला ....
translated_text: This Ashish Shelar has become poor, oh my God.... what has he started doing....
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the word "दरिद्री" (daridri), meaning "poor" or "impoverished," used in a derogatory manner, implying incompetence and disapproval, thus indicating a negative sentiment.

Text: नौटंकी पप्पूची लायकी आहे का मोदींचे नाव उच्चारण्याची?
translated_text: Is Nautanki Pappu worthy of uttering Modi's name?
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the derogatory term "नौटंकी पप्पूची लायकी" (Nautanki Pappu's worth), which implies that "Pappu" is unworthy.

Text: हे म्हणजे असे आहे बेरोजगारी भारतात नाही भारतीय बेरोजगार आहे.
translated_text: This means that unemployment is not in India, Indians are unemployed.
Justification: The sentence expresses a negative sentiment because it highlights the dire state of unemployment through the phrase "भारतीय बेरोजगार आहे" (Bharatiya berojgar aahe), meaning "Indians are unemployed," which emphasizes individual struggle rather than systemic unemployment.

Text: तुझं धड आहे का आधी ते पोरांना नादाला लावतोय
translated_text: Do you have the guts to beat the boys first?
Justification: "धड आहे का" implies questioning someone's capability or courage, suggesting disapproval and thus negative sentiment.

Text: साहेब हया वेडी कुठे जिले होते हैं तुमचे विचार ?????
translated_text: Sir, where do your thoughts go, you crazy person?????
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the use of "वेडी" (vedi), which translates to "crazy person" and implies an insult. This direct insult expresses anger.

Text: बिस्किट खाणार्या वात्रकार दिवसेंदिवस गरीब होत आहेत
translated_text: Biscuit-eating farmers are getting poorer day by day.
Justification: The phrase "गरीब होत आहेत" (garib hot aahet) meaning "are getting poorer" indicates a negative sentiment.

Text: पासून भरत्या रखडल्या आहेत तेच काय झालं राणे साहेब
translated_text: Recruitment has been stalled since then, what happened, Rane Saheb?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from "रखडल्या आहेत" (rakhadlya aahet), meaning "stalled," indicating a negative situation.

Text: तरीही हा सुधारला आहे का? लातोंके भूत बातोंसे नही मानते
translated_text: Has this improved yet? Latinos don't believe in ghost stories.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the proverb "लातोंके भूत बातोंसे नही मानते" which implies that the subject is stubborn and resistant to change, requiring harsh measures rather than simple words or advice.

Text: नास्रिक. जाणिवपुर्वक करतात हे. बाप तशी बेटी
translated_text: Nasrik. This is what the senses do. Father like daughter
Justification: The negative sentiment is derived from "जाणिवपुर्वक करतात" (janivpurvak karatat), meaning "deliberately/purposely do," implying a negative intention behind the action.

Text: शिव्या खाणे हा अंध भक्तांचा जन्मसिध्द अधिकार आहे का
translated_text: Is it the birthright of blind devotees to be cursed?
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is primarily driven by the word "शिव्या" (shivya), which translates to "curses" or "abuses".

Text: भावपूर्ण श्रद्धांजली..! एक अत्यंत सच्चा, हाडाचा पत्रकार आणि मित्र गमावला...
translated_text: Heartfelt tribute..! A very honest, hardworking journalist and friend has been lost...
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "भावपूर्ण श्रद्धांजली...!" (bhavpurna shraddhanjali...!), which translates to "Heartfelt tribute...!" and is used to express condolences, indicating loss and sadness.

Text: समाजाची शोकांतिका आहे ही
translated_text: This is the tragedy of society.
Justification: The negative sentiment and sadness are driven by the Marathi word "शोकांतिका" (shokantika), which directly translates to "tragedy."

Text: सरकार बंद च करतेय शाळा...आता कोण नाही करत शाळा
translated_text: The government is closing schools...who is not going to school now?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from "बंद च करतेय" (band cha karatey), meaning "is closing down," which expresses a negative action by the government regarding schools.

Text: काय तो विचार काय ती गद्दारी तुमची सगळं कस वोके मध्ये हाय की
translated_text: What is that thought, what is that betrayal, how is everything in your mind?
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "गद्दारी" (gaddari), which translates to "betrayal."

Text: हे व्हायला नाही पाहिजे खरं तर.. कोणी कितीही मोठा का असेना..
translated_text: This shouldn't happen, really.. no matter how big someone is..
Justification: The phrase "नाही पाहिजे खरं तर" (nahi pahije khar tar), meaning "shouldn't happen, really", expresses a strong negative sentiment. The use of "खरं तर" (khar tar) further emphasizes the speaker's disapproval, leading to the negative sentiment.

Text: तु कितीही हाण फावडं.... पण घरातच राहणार घरकोंबडं...
translated_text: No matter how much you throw.... but stay at home, stay home...
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the word "घरकोंबडं" (gharkombda), which literally means "stay-at-home chicken" and implies someone is weak, cowardly, and unwilling to leave the house, thus being insulting.

Text: कारण हा मुलगा आणि नातू बारामतीच्या वळणावर्ती गेला आहे. हिंदुविरोधी
translated_text: Because this son and grandson have gone to the outskirts of Baramati. Anti-Hindu
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "हिंदुविरोधी" (Hinduvirodhi), meaning "Anti-Hindu," which expresses opposition and negativity towards a specific religious group.

Text: हे असे बोलतात जसे फुले आंबडेकर यांच्या बरोबर ह्यांनी काम केले होते
translated_text: They talk as if they had worked with Phule Ambedkar.
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) stems from the phrase "जसे फुले आंबडेकर यांच्या बरोबर ह्यांनी काम केले होते" (jase Phule Ambedkar yanchya barobar hyanni kaam kele hote), which implies disbelief or mockery that the speaker would have worked with such prominent figures. This suggests insincerity and contributes to the negative sentiment.

Text: हृदयावर दगड ठेवा आता, खोट जास्त काळ टिकत नाही
translated_text: Put a stone on your heart now, lies don't last long
Justification: The negative sentiment and anger are primarily driven by the phrase "हृदयावर दगड ठेवा" (hrudayavar dagad theva), which translates to "put a stone on your heart." This idiom implies suppressing one's feelings and enduring something painful, conveying a sense of negativity and potentially anger or resentment towards the situation.

Text: का शेतकऱ्याला दिवाळी आहे का नाही साहेब
translated_text: Why is Diwali celebrated by farmers or not, sir?
Justification: The phrase "का नाही साहेब" (ka nahi saheb) implying doubt and questioning the situation contributes to the negative sentiment.

Text: राजकारणी कसा नसावा याचं उत्तम उदाहरण म्हणजे हा फावडा...
translated_text: This shovel is a perfect example of what a politician should not be...
Justification: The phrase "फावडा" (shovel) used to describe a politician implies incompetence and lack of sophistication, leading to a negative sentiment. The comparison is inherently derogatory in this context.

Text: कपटनिस दररोज आपली पातळी ओलांडत आहे
translated_text: Kaptanis is surpassing our level every day.
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is driven by the phrase "आपली पातळी ओलांडत आहे" (aapli patali olandat aahe), meaning "surpassing our level," which implies a condescending or undermining action, even if presented neutrally. This suggests the subject is acting superior, contributing to the negative connotation.

Text: मीडियावाले आणि विरोधी विचाराचे भक्तांना विरोध म्हणून चित्रपट पहायला जातील..
translated_text: They will go to see the film as a protest against media people and devotees of opposing views.
Justification: The phrase "विरोध म्हणून" (virodh mhanun), meaning "as a protest," indicates a negative sentiment as it implies opposition and disagreement.

Text: त्यामुळे पन डॉलरच अजून घट्ट होणार
translated_text: So the dollar will only get stronger.
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is derived from the phrase "अजून घट्ट होणार" (ajun ghatta honar), meaning "will only get stronger." In this context, further strengthening of the dollar is viewed negatively, likely due to its potential adverse effects on the local economy or purchasing power.

Text: अगदी खरं आहे. किडच आहे भ्रष्टवादी ही महाराष्ट्राला लागलेली मढ्याच्या टाळुवरचं लोणी खाणारे..
translated_text: It's absolutely true. It's the corruption that has gripped Maharashtra, eating the butter off the sheep's tongue.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust emotion are primarily driven by the phrase "मढ्याच्या टाळुवरचं लोणी खाणारे" (madhyachya taluvarche loni khanare), which translates to "eating butter off the sheep's tongue" and implies extreme greed and exploitation of a vulnerable situation, evoking strong feelings of disgust and disapproval.

Text: हे आपल्याला हसविण्यासाठी आहे ना.. आपण किती मस्करी अजून सहन करायची
translated_text: This is to make us laugh, right? How much more mockery do we have to endure?
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust are driven by "किती मस्करी सहन करायची" (kiti maskari sahan karayachi), which expresses frustration and unwillingness to tolerate further mockery. It implies a feeling of being fed up and disgusted with the situation.

Text: ते "गोडबोले" होते म्हणून चवताळले बाकी काही नाही.
translated_text: They were "sweet-talking" so they were just being sarcastic.
Justification: The phrase "गोडबोले" (godbole), meaning "sweet-talking" in this context, implies insincerity and manipulation, thus contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: बिजेपी ला मत म्हणजे पुलवामा मधील शहीद जवानांना मारणाऱ्या अतंकवाद्यांना मत.....
translated_text: A vote for BJP means a vote for the terrorists who killed the martyred soldiers in Pulwama.....
Justification: The phrase "शहीद जवानांना मारणाऱ्या अतंकवाद्यांना मत" (martyred soldiers' killers, terrorists' vote) implies strong disapproval and equates voting for BJP with supporting terrorism, hence negative sentiment and disgust.

Text: तू मर्द आहे का?
translated_text: Are you a man?
Justification: The sentence expresses negative sentiment because the phrase "मर्द आहे का?" (mard aahe ka?) implies a challenge to someone's masculinity, questioning their bravery and strength. This is perceived negatively as it's a confrontational inquiry.

Text: याच्या तोंडून एक शब्द पण नकोसा वाटतो ऐकायला.... विदूषकि आहे हा माणूस
translated_text: I don't want to hear a single word from this guy.... This guy is a clown.
Justification: The sentence expresses a negative sentiment due to the phrase "नकोसा वाटतो ऐकायला" (nakosa vatto aikayala), meaning "I don't want to hear," indicating strong dislike. The word "विदूषकि" (vidushaki), meaning "clown," further reinforces the negative sentiment and contributes to the feeling of disgust.

Text: तोपर्यंत शिंदे गट टिकेल का याची काळजी करा तुम्ही.
translated_text: You should worry about whether the Shinde group will survive until then.
Justification: "काळजी करा" (kalaji kara - worry) indicates a negative sentiment as it expresses concern and apprehension about the Shinde group's survival.

Text: हा एडिट केलेला फोटो गुलामांनीच वायरल केला आहे.
translated_text: This edited photo was made viral by Ghulam himself.
Justification: The sentence carries a negative sentiment (-1) due to the term "गुलामांनी" (Ghulam), which implies a sense of disrespect and negativity in the context. Even though the emotion is neutral, the word itself carries a negative connotation.

Text: बरोबर आहे हे दोघे आपल्या तिजोरी वर नागा सारखे बसले आहेत
translated_text: That's right, these two are sitting on their chests like snakes.
Justification: The phrase "नागा सारखे बसले आहेत" (naga sarkhe basle aahet - sitting like snakes) implies a possessive and potentially threatening or dangerous guarding of their treasury, thus contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: लाज काढली आज उद्धव साहेबांनी पण समोरचा तर नर्लज्ज आहे
translated_text: Uddhav Saheb has shown his shame today, but the person in front of him is shameless.
Justification: "नर्लज्ज आहे" (narlajja aahe), meaning "is shameless," indicates a negative sentiment and elicits disgust due to the lack of moral regard implied.

Text: जापनीज पर्यटक महिलेसोबत तरुणांकडून गैरवर्तन...
translated_text: Japanese tourist woman abused by youths...
Justification: "गैरवर्तन" (gairvartan), meaning abuse or misbehavior, strongly indicates a negative sentiment and evokes sadness.

Text: मिळाली ना आमदारकी, धनगर आरक्षणाच्या नावावरती? समाजाची फसवणूक करुन आता मोदींची गुलामगिरी करा..
translated_text: Did you get the MLA seat in the name of Dhangar reservation? Now you are a slave to Modi by deceiving the society..
Justification: "समाजाची फसवणूक करुन" (samajachi phasanuk karun), meaning "deceiving the society," expresses strong disapproval and is a key indicator of negative sentiment. Combined with "गुलामगिरी" (gulamgiri), meaning "slavery," it further reinforces the disgust directed towards the subject.

Text: मित्र असला तरी प्र वृत्ती नीच आहे असे खुद्द बाळासाहेब म्हणाले होते
translated_text: Balasaheb himself had said that even if you have a friend, your attitude is low.
Justification: The phrase "प्र वृत्ती नीच आहे" (pr vrutti neech aahe), meaning "attitude is low/despicable", indicates a negative sentiment.

Text: एकर जमीन एकट्या गोदरेज ची ती ही मुंबइत
translated_text: An acre of land belongs to Godrej alone, that's Mumbai.
Justification: The sentiment is negative because the phrase "एकर जमीन एकट्या गोदरेज ची ती ही मुंबइत" (Ekar jameen ektya Godrej chi ti hi Mumbait) suggests a concerning level of monopolization/ownership by a single entity (Godrej) in Mumbai, implying inequality or lack of opportunity for others.

Text: आमच्या घराच्या बाहेर ही दगड आहेत बहुतेक उबाठा वाले हल्ला करणार असतील माझ्यावर
translated_text: There are these stones outside our house. Most of the bad guys are going to attack me.
Justification: The phrase "हल्ला करणार असतील माझ्यावर" (halla karnar asteel majhyavar - will attack me) expresses a threat and anticipation of harm, thus indicating fear and a negative sentiment.

Text: मराठी शाळाची पटसंख्या कमी होत नाही इंग्रजी शाळाची पटसंख्या वाढत आहे
translated_text: The number of students in Marathi schools is not decreasing, the number of students in English schools is increasing.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "कमी होत नाही" (kami hot nahi), which means "is not decreasing" (although technically a double negative, the implication in this context is negative because it acknowledges the worry of decreasing numbers).

Text: जनतेला श्ट फरक पडत नाही
translated_text: The public doesn't care.
Justification: "फरक पडत नाही" translates to "doesn't care," indicating indifference and a negative sentiment towards a situation.

Text: असच तुडवणार जनता एक दिवस निव्वळ योगा योग
translated_text: This is how the people will one day destroy pure yoga.
Justification: The negative sentiment and anger are driven by the word "तुडवणार" (tudavnaar), which means "will destroy" or "will trample," indicating a forceful and destructive action by the people.

Text: त्यांनी याचा निषेध म्हणून पक्ष सोडावा
translated_text: They should leave the party in protest.
Justification: The Marathi word "निषेध" (nishedh), meaning "protest," indicates a negative sentiment and triggers the emotion of anger due to its association with disagreement and opposition.

Text: तुम्ही काय शे.. उपटायला विरोधी पक्षात आहेत का
translated_text: What are you doing.. are you in the opposition party to uproot them?
Justification: The phrase "शे.. उपटायला" implies futility and uselessness, contributing to the negative sentiment. "शे.. उपटायला" is an idiom used to express that someone is doing something pointless or unproductive.

Text: ह्यांना तर कोणताही न्युज चँनल घेत नाही... सगळ्यात मोठे कपटी
translated_text: No news channel picks them up... the biggest hypocrite
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the phrase "सगळ्यात मोठे कपटी" (sagalyaat mothe kapati), meaning "the biggest hypocrite," which expresses strong disapproval and moral condemnation.

Text: पराबाचा काका पटेल हे काय पटत नाय
translated_text: What's wrong with Paraba's uncle Patel?
Justification: "पटत नाय" (patat nay), meaning "doesn't seem right" or "isn't acceptable," indicates a negative sentiment and a sense of surprise or disbelief at the situation.

Text: हा कोर्टाचा अपमान आहे
translated_text: This is contempt of court.
Justification: The phrase "अपमान आहे" (apamaan aahe), meaning "is contempt," indicates a negative sentiment and evokes anger due to its accusatory nature.

Text: कीती ती हौस नाकाने कांदे सोलून थोबाड फोडून घेण्याची
translated_text: How dare she peel onions with her nose and smash them in her mouth
Justification: The phrase "थोबाड फोडून घेण्याची" (thobad phodun ghenyachi), meaning "smash them in her mouth," expresses a sense of violence and unpleasantness, driving the negative sentiment and disgust.

Text: ‘मशाली’वरून समता पार्टी आक्रमक; निवडणूक आयोगाकडे धाव, केले   सवाल
translated_text: Samata Party aggressive on 'Mashali'; Runs to Election Commission, questions raised
Justification: The negative sentiment (-1) is driven by the word "आक्रमक" (aakramak), which translates to "aggressive." This term suggests a confrontational and negative stance.

Text: अजित दादांची पक्षात किंमत शून्य होत चालली आहे.. कटू असत
translated_text: Ajit Dada's value in the party is becoming zero.. He was bitter.
Justification: The negative sentiment and sadness are primarily driven by the phrase "किंमत शून्य होत चालली आहे" (kimmat shunya hot chalali aahe), meaning "value is becoming zero," indicating a loss of importance and subsequent sadness in the situation.

Text: राजकीय पोळी भाजण्यासाठी हे राजकारणी हे असे विषय घेतात आण् वाट लावतात सगळ्यांची
translated_text: To fuel political fire, these politicians take up such issues and make everyone wait.
Justification: "वाट लावतात सगळ्यांची" (vaat lavtat saglyanchi - make everyone wait) implies a negative outcome for everyone, indicating a negative sentiment.

Text: मशालीच्या धगीत मूरजीची भुर्जी नक्की होणार होती म्हणून घेतली ना माघार
translated_text: The torchlight procession was sure to be a success, so they withdrew.
Justification: The negative sentiment (Neutral) is attributed to the phrase "माघार घेतली" (maghaar ghetli), meaning "withdrew," which suggests a negative outcome, even if not strongly expressed in the sentence itself.

Text: लटकेंच्या राजीनाम्याचं राजकारण, शिवसेना चिन्हं गोठवणं हा खटाटोप केलाच कशाला?
translated_text: Why the politics of Latke's resignation and the effort to freeze Shiv Sena symbols?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "खटाटोप केलाच कशाला?", which implies a futile and undesirable effort ("खटाटोप" means effort/fuss, and "केलाच कशाला?" expresses a sense of "why bother?" or questioning the purpose of a potentially negative action).

Text: महाराष्ट्रच काय पूर्ण देश तुम्हाला स्वीकारणार नाही
translated_text: Maharashtra alone, the entire country won't accept you.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "स्वीकारणार नाही" (swikarnar nahi), which translates to "won't accept," indicating rejection and contributing to a negative overall feeling.

Text: भारत देशातील कर्तव्य बजावत असतांना शहीद झालेल्या हुतात्म्यांना कोटी कोटी नमन...
translated_text: India pays tribute to the martyrs who lost their lives while performing their duty...
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the phrase "शहीद झालेल्या हुतात्म्यांना" (shahid zhalelya hutatmyanna) which translates to "martyrs who were martyred." This phrase, while respectful, implies death and loss, contributing to the negative sentiment despite the overall positive intention of paying tribute.

Text: आणि येडे अन्धभक्त... दुसऱ्याच्या दरात जबरदस्तीने भजन गात बसतात . ....
translated_text: And these blind devotees... forcefully sing hymns at someone else's expense. ....
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust emotion are primarily driven by the phrase "येडे अन्धभक्त" (yede andhabhakt), which translates to "blind devotees" and carries a derogatory connotation. The word "येडे" (yede) specifically contributes to the negative sentiment, implying foolishness or idiocy.

Text: तील तरूणांनी फक्त भोंगेच लावायचे का?
translated_text: Should young people only blow horns?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the rhetorical question implying that the youth are engaging in trivial activities ("फक्त भोंगेच लावायचे का?"), rather than contributing meaningfully. This suggests disappointment or disapproval.

Text: अरे बीन च्या विमाना तुझी लायकी आहे का असामान्य माणसावर बोलायची?
translated_text: Hey Bean's plane, do you have the right to talk about an extraordinary person?
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "बीन च्या विमाना तुझी लायकी आहे का" (Bean's plane, do you have the right?), which is sarcastic and belittling. This indicates a lack of respect and a negative attitude.

Text: आज या राणेला केंद्रीय मंत्री बनवण्याची नामुष्की आली फसणवीस वर  लाचारी
translated_text: Today, the embarrassment of making this Rane a Union Minister has come upon Fasnavis, helplessness on his part.
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the Marathi word "नामुष्की" (namushki), which translates to "embarrassment" or "disgrace." It implies an undesirable and negative situation.

Text: आणि तुम्ही ज्या मार्गाने जात आहेत तो लाचारी चा किशोर महामार्ग आहे.
translated_text: And the path you are taking is the juvenile highway of helplessness.
Justification: The sentence expresses a negative sentiment due to the phrase "लाचारी चा किशोर महामार्ग" (lachari cha kishor mahamarg), which translates to "juvenile highway of helplessness." This phrase implies a path characterized by subservience and lack of power, conveying a negative evaluation.

Text: हा थापा बाळासाहेबांचा निकटवर्ती वाटतो यांना?
translated_text: Does this Thapa seem close to Balasaheb?
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from the use of "थापा" (Thapa), which implies the person is a fraud or a fake, hence questioning his proximity to Balasaheb.

Text: लटके वहिनीनी नोकरी वाचवली अभिनंदन... प्रमोद सावंत आता बळीचा पेंग्विन... जय कामाख्या देवी
translated_text: Congratulations to the hanging sister-in-law who saved her job... Pramod Sawant is now the victim's penguin... Hail Kamakhya Devi
Justification: "बळीचा पेंग्विन" (victim's penguin) implies Pramod Sawant is a scapegoat, carrying a negative connotation despite the earlier congratulatory tone, overriding the positive aspects and leading to an overall negative sentiment.

Text: आज की ताजा खबर केसरकरांचे बोल भक्तांना मीरच्या झोंबल्या आणि ते लागलें बोंबल्या.
translated_text: Today's latest news: Kesarkar's words were a source of joy to the devotees and they were immediately shocked.
Justification: The phrase "मीरच्या झोंबल्या आणि ते लागलें बोंबल्या" suggests a sudden negative reaction and shock, with "झोंबल्या" indicating a sharp, stinging sensation/shock, and "लागलें बोंबल्या" implying an outburst or loud complaint due to that shock. This sudden shift from presumed joy to shocked complaint implies negativity and surprise.

Text: राहा मग असला अवस्था मध्ये आमचं काय तुम्हालाच त्रास होणार आहे
translated_text: Stay, then in this situation, what are we going to do to you?
Justification: The negative sentiment and fear emotion stem from the phrase "त्रास होणार आहे" (traas honar aahe), which translates to "you will be troubled/harmed." This indicates a potential negative consequence for the listener, inducing fear.

Text: त्यांच्या प्रतिपादन किंवा प्रति पादनाला किंमत आहे हीच मोठी गंमत आहे
translated_text: The great joke is that their assertion or counter-evidence has value.
Justification: "गंमत आहे" (gamat aahe), meaning "is a joke/fun", is being used sarcastically here. Given the context and sentiment -1, it implies a negative feeling, not genuine joy, rendering "गंमत आहे" as the phrase driving the emotion away from joy despite the literal translation of the word.

Text: कोण बोलल ? तुमच्यात दम होता तर लढायच होत ना..शेळपटासारख मान खाली का घातली
translated_text: Who will speak? If you had the strength, you would have fought..why did you bow your head like a sheep?
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "शेळपटासारख मान खाली का घातली" (shelpatasarkh maan khali ka ghatli) which translates to "why did you bow your head like a sheep?". This implies weakness and lack of courage, contributing to the negative sentiment.

Text: तू काय लावारीस भक्तीनं आहे
translated_text: What are you asking for out of devotion?
Justification: The sentiment is negative because "लावारीस" (lavaris), meaning ownerless/unfounded, implies a lack of reason or basis for the devotion, thus questioning its validity in a dismissive way. This casts a negative light on the action described.

Text: कबुतरे सोडूनच पाकिस्तान बनवला होता.
translated_text: Pakistan was created by abandoning pigeons.
Justification: "सोडूनच" (sodunach) implying abandonment or leaving behind, carries a negative connotation that contributes to the negative sentiment (-1), even if the overall emotion is neutral as it suggests a loss or sacrifice in the creation of Pakistan.

Text: हे शाळा पण विकून खातील
translated_text: They will sell this school too.
Justification: The phrase "विकून खातील" (vikun khatil), meaning "will sell and eat/consume," implies a destructive or exploitative action, leading to the negative sentiment.

Text: ही घटना मनाला अस्वस्थ करणारी आहे.
translated_text: This incident is disturbing.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust emotion are primarily driven by the Marathi phrase "मनाला अस्वस्थ करणारी" (manala asthast karanari), which translates to "disturbing to the mind" or "making the mind uneasy". This phrase directly expresses a negative and unsettling feeling, aligning with both negativity and disgust.

Text: काल पासून हा दुसरा घावलाय
translated_text: This is the second injury since yesterday.
Justification: "घावलाय" (ghavalay), meaning "injury," indicates a negative situation, leading to a negative sentiment even if the emotion is neutral.

Text: आता या घोटाळ्यावर तो तोत्र्या बोलणार नाही काही
translated_text: Now he won't babble on about this scandal.
Justification: The negative sentiment stems from "घोटाळ्यावर तो तोत्र्या बोलणार नाही," implying the subject is avoiding speaking about the scandal, suggesting guilt or complicity. The phrase carries a negative connotation.

Text: आला शिव्या खायला
translated_text: Came to get scolded.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust are driven by the phrase "शिव्या खायला" (shivya khayla), which translates to "to get scolded" and implies an unpleasant and unwanted experience.

Text: मार खाण्याची पण हौस येते का तुला..
translated_text: Do you even feel like getting beaten?
Justification: The phrase "मार खाण्याची पण हौस येते का" (maar khaanyachi pan haus yete ka), specifically "हौस येते का" (haus yete ka) which translates to "do you feel like it/do you have a liking for it?" in the context of getting beaten, expresses disgust and disbelief, implying the speaker finds the idea repulsive.

Text: तुम्ही गददारांचे वारसदारबाप पण गद्दार आणि तू पण
translated_text: You are the heirs of traitors, but you are also traitors, and you too
Justification: The phrase "गददारांचे वारसदार" (gad'darānche vārasdār), meaning "heirs of traitors," strongly indicates a negative sentiment and evokes disgust by associating the subject with betrayal and treachery. Furthermore, calling someone "गद्दार" (gad'dar) directly reinforces the disgust and negativity.

Text: लवकरच या तथाकथित महाशक्ती म्हणवून घेणाऱ्यांना उतरती कळा लागणार हे निश्चित..
translated_text: It is certain that soon these so-called superpowers will have to face a setback.
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by the phrase "उतरती कळा लागणार" (utarti kala lagnar), which translates to "will face a setback" or "will have a decline," indicating a negative future outcome for the subject. The phrase "तथाकथित" (tathakathit) "so-called" add to the negative sentiment .

Text: जपान साठी सहानभुती आणि अमेरिका साठी
translated_text: Sympathy for Japan and for America
Justification: The sentiment is negative due to the incomplete and abruptly ending sentence "अमेरिका साठी" which, in this context, implies a missing negative element compared to the expression of sympathy for Japan. While not explicitly negative, the lack of a comparable expression for America following the expression of sympathy for Japan creates a negative implication or bias.

Text: तुम्ही काय लिहिले मला काही दिसतं नाही....गेली का तुमच्या एसी ची लाईट...
translated_text: I can't see what you wrote....did your AC light go out...
Justification: The negative sentiment is driven by "काही दिसतं नाही" (kahi disat nahi), meaning "can't see anything," indicating frustration or problem causing a negative experience.

Text: भक्त ला हा शेनक्या त्रास देतोय शाळा घेतली पाहिजे
translated_text: This skunk is bothering the devotee, he should go to school.
Justification: The negative sentiment and disgust emotion are driven by the word "शेनक्या" (shenkya), which is a derogatory term for "skunk". The sentence implies the subject is undesirable and contemptible.

Text: फार चुकीचे काम करत आहेत , हि खोके मंडळी .
translated_text: These idiots are doing something very wrong.
Justification: "फार चुकीचे" (far chukiche), meaning "very wrong" coupled with "खोके मंडळी" (khoke mandali), which translates to "idiots" and implies corruption, strongly indicates a negative sentiment and evokes anger.
